replies.author: @roluevasreisa5730
replies.text:
The life cycle of a call of duty player 

Trailer comes out . Activision slaps fans 

Fans: OH MY god! Why did you just slap me? 

Activision: Yeah? What are you going to do about it? 

Fans: im going to.. not be happy about it! 

Activision: Ok wuss.

Activision slaps the fans again 

Fans: Stop slapping me!! 

Activision: What are you going to do to stop me? Go cry to mommy 

Fans: Thats it!!! We are boycotting!! 

Activision: Yeah you do that 

Fans: We are serious! We are super duper going to boycott! Im serious! 

Activision: cool story. See you in the fall 

Game release time. Fans come in

Activision: WELL WELL WELL

Fans: Hi we would like 

Activision spits on Fans and then slaps them in the face 

Activision: Please continue

Fans: buy five copies each of the latest call of duty game 

Activision spits on copies slaps fans in the face and hands them the games 

Activision: Here you go. See you next year loosers

author: @LeeONardo
text:
COD 4 was what brought gaming to where it is today, for the worse.
Publishers started releasing an oversaturation of military games, browns and grays, FPS all over the place. Japanese devs started using western devs for franchises and ruining them all as well as making heavily western style games at the expense of their own, all publishers wanted multiple millions of copies sold before they say a game as being a success, game originality took a nosedive that only recovered thanks to indie games a long time later.

COD 4 is a great game, though to me Black Ops 1 was better, I never played any after it.

author: @Hawkeye26
text:
13:20 Speaking as someone who never got into either the Battlefield nor the COD games, I enjoyed watching people like AZ (from HeelvsBabyface) playing Battlefield 6, and was intrigued when I learned that a single player version of gameplay would be made available, it told me that the game could be a modern day Unreal Tournament type game, and I'm very intrigued. The graphics look amazing, and AZ made it look fun (it was just a lil too intense for me, I'd get decimated trying to play as part of some random online team of people against another online team of people), but if I'm right in what I've been reading up about the game...I might be interested in playing it when it comes out properly.

I felt it important to put this here, because I support Jeremy as a regular G+G viewer, and represent the element of the population that is being referenced at the point of the timestamp provided.

author: @HULK-HOGAN1
text: My crew started playing COD with the legendary COD 4. We played full time through to MW3. Then we took a massive break until the Modern Warfare 2019 reboot which was genuinely awesome. Unfortunately the studios copied, pasted, cut campaigns, ruined map rotations with uninspired thoughtless AI generated maps, manipulated gameplay with egregious algorithms like SBMM and EOMM, stupid battle pass, chat censorship and fortnitification. We're buying BF6 and will never touch COD again until it goes back to heavy military themes, ideally set around 2005 before AI and the general tech overload.

author: @DamianHowardTV
text:
Last time I played a Call of Duty game or was remotely interested in the franchise, was Modern Warfare 3 back in 2011. Call of Duty hasn’t been relevant or good since then. It’s been over-blown, over-hyped and over-rated. I just simply don’t care about the franchise.
All they’ve tried to do the last few years, is to be “the next big thing” in the Battle Royale market. Trying to be PUBG or a more realistic/grounded Fortnite. Losing everything (incl. the originality) that once made Call of a Duty great.

author: @shonen_x_trash2488
text: Hey Jeremy, I unsubbed and this will be my last comment. I respectfully want to explain why before I go. On G&G 2 channel you hosted a discussion about the switch 2 where I felt like you shot down the other panelists criticism. This annoyed me, but this isn't why I'm unsubbing. I'm unsubbing cause when myself and others voiced our dislike of this, you were as obnoxious as a millenial comic book writer treating your disagreeing subscribers with derision. I do not stand for this, from you or other creators: mainstream or counter culture. There are a lot of channels that cover this content, and I'd rather watch someone who doesn't act like a 12 year old when people who watch his content disagrees with him. I was subbed to multiple channels of yours and now I will tell the algorithm to avoid your content. I am posting this comment so that you and your team can understand the position of myself and others and course correct before it's too late. Thank you for the entertainment throughout the years, and goodbye.

author: @Fxstarlord
text:
Battlefield, same game same theme, same story, same jets, same helicopters, same building destruction,  same looking solder, same load outside, same plane modern warfare.  
Cod same foundation,  different guns, different theme, different movement, scores scorestreaks, executions. Different type of characters because different type of people like different type of stuff but still want to play cod. Maps, warzone, zombies, zombie maps, zombie storyline, multiple games connecting to make one big universe, different collabs. 
But no, everyone thinks Battlefield will beat cod. Battlefield!!? Lol they've been fucking us since after Battlefield 3. I play both because sometimes I just want pull away from everything a game can give me and just want a regular war game.
